body.probe: Igor emits a prediction note alongside a deferred fetch; next turn shows DEFERRED_RESULT and anticipation.py logs a match/mismatch signal
body.title: Igor guesses before fetching — prediction vs actual drives reward
body.result: compare_prediction_to_result() (Jaccard word overlap, threshold 0.15). evaluate_deferred_predictions() scans TWM for deferred_prediction + deferred_self_task pairs after each completions drain: MATCH→milieu ingest_resolution_reward + PREDICTION_MATCH ring, MISMATCH→PREDICTION_MISMATCH ring. Prediction notes tagged source=deferred_prediction at push time. 19 tests, 1800 total green.
body.description: Build on T-igor-deferred-self-tasks: before fetching context (memory_search, twm_read, etc.), Igor emits a prediction of what he expects to find as a DEFERRED_TASK|note|prediction:... alongside the fetch task. On the next turn both his prediction and the actual result are in TWM. anticipation.py compares them: if match → resolution_reward (positive); if mismatch → surprise_delta → NE deeper processing. This trains the word graph + anticipation circuit through normal usage — self-generated supervised learning.
